title: 2025 Session 5 exercises
body:
Session 5: Exercises


Question 1
The interest rate on one-year risk-free bonds is 5% in the United Kingdom and 3.75% in
Switzerland. The current exchange rate is £0.5 per Swiss franc. Suppose that you are a
British investor and you expect the foreign currency (Swiss franc) to appreciate by 2%
over the next year.
     a. Calculate the foreign currency risk premium, which is defined as the difference
         between the expected exchange rate return E(S) and the interest rate differential.
     b. Based on your result, does the uncovered interest rate parity hold?
     c. Calculate the domestic currency return on the foreign bond, assuming that your
         currency appreciation expectations are met.
Question 2
You believe that the U.S. dollar will strongly appreciate against the euro in the next few
weeks, much beyond what is currently priced in the forward rate. What action can you
take?
Question 3
On June 30, 2024, a company bought a one-year forward contract for receiving USD 1
million for AUD 1.50 million (AUD 1.50 per USD).
   a. Calculate the payoff if the spot exchange rate on June 30, 2025 is AUD 1.55 per
      USD.
   b. Calculate the payoff if the spot exchange rate on June 30, 2025 is AUD 1.45 per
      USD.
Question 4
Assume that we have:
             Spot exchange rate:           AUD 1.50 per USD
             One-year forward rate:              AUD 1.55 per USD
             Risk-free interest rate in USD:     3% (annualized)
             Risk-free interest rate in AUD:     5% (annualized)

   a. A company has to pay a cost of USD 1 million to its supplier next year. To better
      hedge the exchange risk, what can the company do (purchase forward contracts or
      hedge by money market transactions)?
   b. A company will receive a revenue of USD 1 million from its customer next year.
      To better hedge the exchange risk, what can the company do (purchase forward
      contracts or hedge by money market transactions)?




                                             1
 Question 5
Suppose that the forward ask price for March 20 on euros is $0.9127 at the same time that
the price of IMM euro futures for delivery on March 20 is $0.9145. How could an
arbitrageur profit from this situation?
